name: DolEx Dollar Express
description_short: DolEx is a multi-service financial services provider offering check cashing, money transfers, bill pay, money orders, and personal loans through Oportun at thousands of locations across 20+ countries.
description_long: DolEx operates as a comprehensive financial services hub targeting underbanked and immigrant communities, positioning itself as a one-stop shop for essential financial needs. The company maintains thousands of physical locations across the U.S. and international markets, with over 125,000 payers active in more than 20 countries, indicating significant scale in the money transfer segment. Their service portfolio has expanded well beyond traditional check cashing to include international money transfers, bill payment processing, telephony top-ups, money orders, and personal loans offered through a partnership with Oportun (a CDFI lender). Tax preparation services are also available, though limited to New York locations. DolEx's primary distinguishing factor is the breadth of services consolidated into single locations, allowing customers to handle multiple financial tasks without visiting multiple providers. The company emphasizes convenience and accessibility for populations that may lack traditional banking relationships, with a stated mission of "connecting families in the U.S. and around the world." However, as a check-cashing and money services business rather than a bank, DolEx does not offer FDIC-insured deposit accounts, and the availability of specific services varies significantly by location and state, which may limit utility for some consumers.
pros: Thousands of physical locations provide local accessibility for check cashing and money transfers
Multi-service consolidation allows customers to handle check cashing, bill pay, money orders, and transfers in one visit
International money transfer network spanning 20+ countries with established payer base of 125,000+
Personal loan options available up to $10,000 through Oportun partnership
Bill pay service covers 10,000+ utility companies including mortgage, cable, and phone providers
Telephony services (pinless top-ups, domestic and international) integrated into same locations
Mobile app available for online access to services
cons: Service availability varies significantly by location and state, creating inconsistent consumer experience
No deposit accounts or FDIC insurance—operates as money services provider, not bank
Website lacks transparent fee disclosure for check cashing, money transfers, or other services
Tax preparation limited to New York only, restricting utility for most customers
Personal loans require partnership with Oportun; terms, rates, and APR not disclosed on DolEx site
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers needing check cashing without traditional bank accounts
Immigrants and families sending money internationally to 20+ countries
Customers seeking consolidated financial services (check cashing, bill pay, money orders) in single location
diagnosis: DolEx is best for unbanked and underbanked consumers, particularly immigrants and families needing check cashing and international money transfers at convenient physical locations. The main caveat is that service availability is highly inconsistent across locations and states, and the company does not offer deposit accounts or banking services—it is strictly a money services provider with non-transparent fee structures.
services: Bill payment (10,000+ utility companies, mortgage, cable, phone)
Location finder and payout location search
